commit.message:
Consolidate repo guidance into AGENTS.md (#16)

* Consolidate repo guidance into AGENTS.md

- Move content from .openhands/microagents/repo.md to AGENTS.md
- Create symlink .openhands/skills/repo.md -> ../../AGENTS.md
- Create symlink CONTRIBUTING.md -> AGENTS.md (if not existing)

This establishes AGENTS.md as the single source of truth for repository guidance.

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Remove legacy AGENTS.md symlinks

Symlinks to AGENTS.md caused agent instructions to be loaded twice; replace with small pointer files.

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Remove legacy guidance files

Remove legacy-path guidance files to avoid duplicated instructions; AGENTS.md is the single source of truth.

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

---------

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

commit.message:
fix typo in prompt that will break task_type (#11)

* fix typo in prompt

* be more lenient for missing fields

* rubrics: let FeatureData.from_tool_args exceptions bubble up so tests and CI catch invalid tool args; remove unused ValidationError import and noisy logging\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Revert "rubrics: let FeatureData.from_tool_args exceptions bubble up so tests and CI catch invalid tool args; remove unused ValidationError import and noisy logging\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>"

This reverts commit 000ad3b386d8c62e748329dd358571bc506a0661.

* tests: align expectations with lenient parsing in BaseRubrics.tool_call_to_feature_data\n- invalid JSON returns empty feature list\n- missing fields skip features instead of raising\n- invalid classification label skips feature instead of raising\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

---------

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

commit.message:
docs: rewrite README and add batch annotation guide (#8)

* docs: rewrite README and add batch annotation guide

- Replace marketing-focused README with technical documentation
- Focus on core data structures (BasePrediction, Feature, FeatureData, BaseRubrics)
- Document all key APIs with method signatures and usage examples
- Add comprehensive batch annotation workflow guide in docs/BATCH_ANNOTATE.md
- Include complete working examples and data flow diagrams
- Cover 3-step pipeline: send requests → download results → consolidate data

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Update README.md

---------

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

commit.message:
refactor Rubric to accept a list of Features as input (#7)

* add script to generate annotation requests for debug

* rename script

* minor tweak symbols

* tweak to fix test

* remaining test fix

* improve converter to handle the last turn

* replace unicode dot with -

* improve script that convert annotation result

* feat(annotators): add LiteLLM-backed Annotator with real-time and batch APIs; type-safety for pyright; ruff clean

- Implement Annotator.annotate (real-time) and batch_annotate (LiteLLM Batches API)
- Build NDJSON in-memory, upload via create_file, create_batch, optionally parse outputs if completed
- Tighten types: Literal endpoint/completion_window, casts for batch returns

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* annotators/Annotator: retry single and batch with tenacity; batch_annotate no polling + sequential custom_id + persist inputs/batch metadata; add download_annotation; fix typing; add tenacity dep\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* make results_dir required

* annotators: add chunked batch submission respecting per-batch request/size limits; introduce results_dir manifest and per-batch subdirs; add aggregate download helper

This keeps the previous single-batch API for backward compatibility and adds batch_annotate_chunked to split requests by count/bytes and persist per-batch metadata under results_dir/batches.

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* remove backward compatibility for simplicity

* add initial annotation util function implmentation

* remove timing on traj w/o user follow-up

* refactor base rubrics to use features

* add the unimplemented interface for model response

* complete refactor of rubrics to use Features

* remove ah_data as dependency

* remove mixin to simplify implementation for now

* revert changes to prediction

* minor tweaks to prompt

* add tests to compare rubrics

* remove more weird unicode symbols with common ascii symbols

* fix: it is api_base instead of base_url

* fix issue for create batch

* fix remaining bugs

* remove temperature bc o3 doesn't support it

* set default temperature to 0

* remove metadata when it is done

* support save inputs file to output dir

* fix pop metadata field again

* fix download script

* fix to download error result too

* Improve progress visualization in download annotations script

- Add permanent progress log that persists after Progress context exits
- Capture all progress events (completed, failed, pending, errors) in progress_log
- Display processing log with color-coded status messages after download completes
- Users can now see what happened during the download process even after it finishes
- Fixes issue where progress information completely disappeared when task finished

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* fix metadata field

* set model-specific arguments/reasoning effort

* rename folder to outputs

* log path for outputs and error

* Implement FeatureData conversion and batch consolidation system

- Add FeatureData class in critic_rubrics/feature.py with from_tool_args() method
- Implement tool_calls_to_feature_data() method on BaseRubrics class
- Add type checking and argument extraction for BinaryPrediction, TextPrediction, ClassificationPrediction
- Create comprehensive unit tests in tests/test_feature_data_conversion.py using real batch data
- Implement 3_consolidate_and_convert_outputs.py script to aggregate and convert batch annotation outputs
- Add JSON parsing and error handling for tool call arguments
- Successfully tested with real batch output files from data/annotated_traces/

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Refactor: Rename tool_calls_to_feature_data to model_response_to_feature_data

- Updated BaseRubrics method to accept ModelResponse instead of raw tool_calls
- Added proper error handling for missing choices, messages, or tool_calls
- Updated consolidation script to use new method with ModelResponse objects
- Updated all tests to use new method signature with helper method
- Added pytest as dev dependency
- Fixed type checking issues with type ignore comments

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* some style preferences

* Simplify batch consolidation script - eliminate 50% of code

- Combine file loading and tool call extraction into single generator
- Replace hasattr() with proper isinstance() type checking
- Inline simple operations and eliminate unnecessary function boundaries
- Reduce verbose logging to essential information only
- Reduce from 337 lines to 164 lines while maintaining identical functionality

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* significantly simply feature serialization via class method on prediction class

* remove abstract method

* use custom exception PredictionMissingFieldError

* we detect rubric class on the fly

* further simplify type name

* Update tests to work with new tool_call_to_feature_data API

- Updated method calls to pass individual tool calls instead of ModelResponse
- Fixed test expectations to handle strict validation (exceptions for missing/invalid fields)
- Updated error handling tests to expect exceptions instead of empty results
- All tests now pass with the new tool_call_match_rubrics implementation

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* Improve exception handling in tests with proper pytest.raises

- Replace generic try/except Exception blocks with specific pytest.raises
- Use PredictionMissingFieldError for missing field tests
- Use ValidationError for invalid value tests
- Import specific exception types for better type safety
- Cleaner, more maintainable test code

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

---------

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

commit.message: cleanup openhands repo.md
reduce length to 200
empty test folder
add initial trajectory and remove annotators
improve baseclass
initial version of AnnotateConversationRubrics
renmae pre-commit config
re-initialize repo workflow and dependency management

rubrics: add BaseRubrics.get_tool_schema() for dynamic flattened tool schema; implement TrajectoryUserFollowupRubrics using BaseRubrics and add default_factory for all Prediction fields to make model robust to partial tool outputs

- BaseRubrics.get_tool_schema() builds <field>_{detected,rationale} and merges extra_tool_properties(); REQUIRED_ALL defaults to False
- TrajectoryUserFollowupRubrics declares 23 Prediction fields + extras in schema (follow_up_timing, follow_up_timing_rationale, task_type)
- Annotator already uses dynamic schema and parses flattened keys

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>
annotators: robust import guarding and default-fill all Prediction fields on parse to satisfy model validation\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

rubrics: add BaseRubrics with dynamic get_tool_schema(); implement TrajectoryUserFollowupRubrics with extras and REQUIRED_ALL=False; update TrajectoryAnnotator to use flattened schema parsing; guard optional annotator imports\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

pkg: guard optional imports in rubrics/__init__.py and top-level __init__.py; export BaseRubrics and TrajectoryUserFollowupRubrics

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

rubrics: add BaseRubrics with dynamic get_tool_schema; implement TrajectoryUserFollowupRubrics with extras (follow_up_timing, rationale, task_type); remove static ANNOTATION_TOOL in trajectory_with_user_followup.py.

Note: __init__.py still imports optional modules; may need guards if importing package in isolation.

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>
tmp copy around
tmp
simplify

Add comprehensive BatchProcessor for multi-provider batch processing

MAJOR FEATURES:
- Enhanced BaseAnnotator with get_request() method for request inspection
- Full backward compatibility: supports both string and structured request formats
- BatchProcessor class with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and async processing
- Provider-agnostic batch file creation and submission
- Comprehensive batch status checking and result downloading
- Rate-limited async processing for real-time requests

TECHNICAL DETAILS:
- BaseAnnotator.get_request(): Generate litellm completion format without execution
- BaseAnnotator.annotate(): Refactored to use get_request() internally
- BatchProcessor.to_openai_batch_request(): Convert to OpenAI JSONL batch format
- BatchProcessor.to_anthropic_batch_request(): Convert to Anthropic Request format
- BatchProcessor.submit_batch(): Submit batches to provider APIs
- BatchProcessor.check_batch_status(): Monitor batch processing progress
- BatchProcessor.download_batch_results(): Parse and return typed results
- BatchProcessor.process_async(): Async processing with rate limiting

COMPATIBILITY:
- Handles both 'messages_for_annotator' + 'tools_for_annotator' format (research scripts)
- Maintains string input support for simple use cases
- All existing tests pass - no breaking changes

EXAMPLES:
- Comprehensive batch_processing_example.py demonstrating all features
- Shows request format inspection, batch creation, provider comparison
- Generated batch files match OpenAI API specification exactly

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

Add comprehensive system messages from original Xingyao rubrics

- Added system message support to BaseAnnotator with _get_system_message() method
- TrajectoryAnnotator: Complete system message with all rubric definitions and examples
  * User follow-up patterns with mutual exclusivity rules
  * Agent behavioral issues with detailed examples
  * Infrastructure issues (external vs agent-caused)
  * Quality standards and evidence requirements
- SolvabilityAnnotator: Comprehensive system message for issue analysis
  * Problem definition factors
  * Technical details requirements
  * Context and scope analysis
  * Evidence-based evaluation standards
- System messages are now properly included in LLM calls
- All tests pass: schema consistency maintained

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

Complete TrajectoryRubrics with all 23 features from original Xingyao rubrics

  * 13 agent behavioral issues (misunderstood_intention, insufficient_analysis, etc.)
  * 2 infrastructure issues (external vs agent-caused)
- Updated TrajectoryAnnotator to generate schema for all 23 fields
- Added comprehensive unit test (test_schema_consistency.py) to verify annotator schemas match rubric dataclasses
- Updated examples and README to reflect complete feature set
- All tests pass: schemas are consistent between annotators and rubrics

Restructure package: rename to critic_rubrics with modular architecture

Major changes:
- Rename package from unified_rubrics to critic_rubrics
- Reorganize into modular structure with separate folders for annotators
- Create dataclasses for each rubric type (SolvabilityRubrics, TrajectoryRubrics, ConversationRubrics)
- Implement Prediction dataclass with bool + rationale structure
- Add comprehensive trajectory rubrics with superset of features
- Separate solvability and trajectory annotators into their own folders
- Update all imports, tests, and documentation
- Add new comprehensive examples and test coverage

Architecture:
- critic_rubrics/rubrics/: Rubric dataclasses
- critic_rubrics/annotators/: Modular annotator implementations
- critic_rubrics/core.py: Core classes including Prediction
- Comprehensive test suite with 3 test modules

Features:
- 12 solvability features with detection rates and helper methods
- 42+ trajectory features covering agent behavior, user patterns, technical issues
- Multi-sample analysis with consensus and diversity metrics
- Batch processing capabilities
- Extensible base annotator class

Add unified rubrics system

This commit introduces a comprehensive unified rubrics system that consolidates
three different rubric implementations from All-Hands-AI research projects:

1. Calvin Featurizer - LLM-based feature extraction for solvability analysis
2. Xingyao Rubrics - Agent behavior analysis and conversation annotation
3. Custom Rubrics - Flexible framework for domain-specific evaluation

Key Features:
- Flexible rubric system with categories, items, and additional fields
- Multiple LLM backend support (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc. via LiteLLM)
- Multi-sample analysis for robust statistical evaluation
- Batch processing for efficient parallel annotation
- Pre-built rubrics for common use cases
- Extensible design for custom rubric types

Package Structure:
- core.py: Core classes and interfaces (RubricItem, RubricSet, etc.)
- rubrics.py: Pre-defined rubric sets and builders
- annotators.py: LLM annotator implementations
- examples.py: Comprehensive usage examples
- tests/: Test suite for all functionality

Documentation:
- README.md: Package documentation and usage guide
- MIGRATION_GUIDE.md: Migration instructions from existing systems
- UNIFIED_RUBRICS_PROPOSAL.md: Detailed design and architecture

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>
